top_performers.notes: Conceptual pitch, paradigm shift framing, signal vs surface metrics
Philosophical contrast noise vs systems, reframed technical points
Contrarian hook challenging follower size assumption, bullet structure
Framed Rally as infrastructure not platform, conceptual take

overused_phrases.note: Mandatory topic but find unique framing
Important detail, include but don't center

winning_patterns: Reframe technical points into narrative/conceptual framing
Personal voice with distinct perspective, not corporate tone
Challenge status quo of web3 marketing measurement
Stream-of-consciousness or self-doubt elements for authenticity
unique_gaps: Nobody frames Rally as solving a SPECIFIC creator pain point with a personal story
Nobody uses analogy/metaphor from outside crypto
Nobody writes about the EXPERIENCE of discovering Rally (first-person journey)
Nobody questions whether AI scoring could be gamed (and why it's hard)
Nobody compares Rally to specific traditional platforms they've used
common_failures: 70% get Originality 1/2 - AI-sounding language, template structure
83% get Engagement 4/5 or less - generic hooks, weak CTAs
Most use same angles: quality>followers, being early, AI scoring

calibration.competition_level: HIGH - 30% get max Originality, 17% get max Engagement
